title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Secret Life of Mycorrhizal Networks
content.text: Beneath the soil of every forest lies a complex biological network that (0) connects trees in a silent, subterranean web. Known as the "wood wide web," these mycorrhizal networks are (1) of fungi that form symbiotic relationships with tree roots. For decades, ecologists (2) that trees were solitary competitors, fighting for sunlight and space. However, recent research has (3) this view, revealing that trees actually share nutrients and information through these fungal threads. Older "mother trees" can even (4) their own offspring and send them extra carbon to help them survive in the shaded understory. This cooperation is (5) to the health of the entire forest ecosystem, especially during periods of drought or disease. When a tree is under attack by insects, it can (6) chemical signals through the network to warn its neighbors, allowing them to boost their natural defenses. While scientists are still (7) with the full complexity of these interactions, it is clear that forests are far more than just collections of individual plants. They are highly integrated communities where the survival of one (8) on the strength of the whole, a discovery that has fundamentally changed our understanding of plant biology and the intricate balance of the natural world.

example.explanation: “Connects” is the most natural verb for describing a network that links different entities.

questions.explanation: “Composed of” is the standard formal collocation meaning to be made up of something.
“Assumed” means to accept something as true without proof, common in historical scientific contexts.
“Challenged a view” is a strong academic collocation for questioning an established theory.
“Recognize” means to identify from previous knowledge, used here for biological identification.
“Vital to” is a fixed collocation meaning essential for something’s success or existence.
“Transmit signals” is the precise technical term for sending information or data.
“Grappling with” is an idiom meaning to try hard to understand or deal with a difficult problem.
“Hinges on” is a phrasal verb meaning to depend entirely on something.
